title: Lycaste panamanensis
taxonomy: Lycaste panamanensis is a species of orchid. Lycaste panamanensis belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a genus within the subtribe Maxillariinae. Lycaste panamanensis is a type of terrestrial orchid that is native to Central America. The species is known for its unique and vibrant flowers.

It is native to Central America and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Lycaste panamanensis is a species of orchid, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lycaste panamanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Lycaste panamanensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory infections.
